posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The U.S. is actively monitoring vessel traffic through an alternative Omani shipping route to maintain crude and LPG supply flows through the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting efforts to sustain energy exports despite regional security concerns.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister is warning that deviating from its current approach could complicate efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and escalate tensions, with potential implications for global oil supply flows through this critical chokepoint. The statement suggests Iran may resist alternative arrangements that differ from its existing framework for managing the strait.

posts.text:
Oil and Gas Investment and Field Decline Rates

Global oil and gas producers need to invest more than $500 billion each year just to sustain output in the face of natural declines from existing fields as pressure falls, according to estimates prepared by the International Energy https://t.co/67gXySweOJ
posts.summary: Global oil and gas producers must spend over $500 billion annually just to maintain current output levels as existing fields naturally decline, according to IEA estimates. This substantial investment requirement to sustain production underscores the ongoing capital intensity of the oil industry amid depletion pressures.

posts.text: Senior US officials frustrated by Machado bid to return to Venezuela after quakes, White House official says https://t.co/8QOihLvVWK
posts.summary: Senior US officials are frustrated with opposition leader Machado's attempt to return to Venezuela following recent earthquakes, according to a White House official. The political tensions could complicate US-Venezuela relations and potentially affect oil market dynamics given Venezuela's significant crude reserves and existing US sanctions on Venezuelan oil exports.

posts.text:
Global energy spending is on track to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026

Around $2.2 trillion of this is set to go to grids, storage, low-emissions fuels, nuclear, renewables, efficiency & electrification

And about $1.2 trillion goes to oil, natural gas & coal: https://t.co/8IajZj1gIL https://t.co/yLRuHSwqq9
posts.summary: Global energy spending is projected to reach $3.4 trillion in 2026, with approximately $1.2 trillion allocated to fossil fuels including oil, natural gas, and coal. This represents a smaller share compared to the $2.2 trillion directed toward clean energy and grid infrastructure.

posts.text:
A huge surplus in global oil markets going into the Hormuz crisis provided a major cushion against the initial shock

But with global oil inventories falling at a record pace since then, the recovery in flows through the Strait has come at a critical time: https://t.co/vvjkiIlFD9 https://t.co/fUbLuwuQLl
posts.summary: Global oil inventories are depleting at record rates following the Hormuz crisis, making the recent recovery in strait flows critical for stabilizing markets that had previously benefited from a significant surplus cushion.
